title: Understanding the Problem of Amnesia in AI Agents
summary: Explores the issue of AI agents forgetting information when a session ends, and the need for a persistent memory solution. Introduces the concept of using multiple databases to address this problem.
transcript: Every agent that I have built, I feel that it's suffering from a problem known as amnesia. You close the tab and your agent forgets everything. You start a new session and it's like meeting a completely new stranger. So where does the problem lie for building these AI agents? We might require multiple databases. Vector DB for embeddings for example pine cone or VV8 relational DB for structured data for example Postgress SQL and document store for unstructured data for example MongoDB. But it is a huge problem. three billing dashboards, three failure points and constant data syncing. Today I'm going to show you how can you build a deep research agent with persistent memory that two with just one database that is Oracle 26 AI AI database and you know the best part it runs on their always free tire. Also before starting this video if you're new to this channel make sure you hit that like button and subscribe to the channel for more such AI content coming ahead and thanks to Oracle for collaborating with me on this content piece. You know the major problem lies in data fragmentation. Let's see what this problem is and how are we going to solve with Oracle 26 AI database. So the problem is data fragmentation. Let's try and understand what data fragmentation is. Let's take a simple example of how our traditional AI agent architecture looks like. Right? We have an AI agent that's connected to multiple databases. For example, we have Pine Cone for vectors. We have Post SQL for metadata and we have MongoDB let's say to store documents.
